rules.title: Use Rust edition 2024
rules.guidance: RustUse crates should use Rust edition 2024 and the workspace Rust toolchain baseline.

rules.title: Facade crates primarily re-export child crates
rules.guidance: A use-* facade should organize and re-export focused child crates instead of holding most implementation.

rules.title: Child crates hold implementation
rules.guidance: Focused child crates should contain the primitive types, functions, tests, and examples for their domain.

rules.title: Prefer primitive utility crates
rules.guidance: RustUse favors small composable primitives over frameworks, platforms, or broad opinionated stacks.

rules.title: Prefer few or no dependencies
rules.guidance: Use the standard library where practical and add dependencies only when they clearly improve correctness or maintenance.

rules.title: Dual license MIT OR Apache-2.0
rules.guidance: RustUse workspaces and crates should use the dual license expression MIT OR Apache-2.0.

rules.title: Ship docs, tests, and examples where appropriate
rules.guidance: Include README, CHANGELOG, rustdoc, tests, and examples at a level proportional to crate risk and scope.

rules.title: Support crates.io dependency adoption
rules.guidance: Published RustUse crates should be usable as normal crates.io dependencies.

rules.title: Support copy-and-own adoption
rules.guidance: Where small primitives are useful as source, preserve a path for users to copy and own code with attribution.

rules.title: Support CLI-assisted adoption
rules.guidance: The rustuse and cargo-rustuse tools may eventually help users select, copy, and track RustUse primitives.

rules.title: Avoid duplicate or crisscrossing taxonomy
rules.guidance: New sets and child crates should avoid colliding names, duplicated concepts, or overlapping ownership boundaries.
